title: Radiation Discoveries
description: Rutherford discovered and named alpha and beta radiation, studying their differing penetrating powers.

title: Radon Characterization
description: While working at McGill University, he characterized and identified the radioactive noble gas, Radon.

title: Nobel Prize in Chemistry
description: Awarded the Nobel Prize in Chemistry for his investigations into the disintegration of elements and the chemistry of radioactive substances.

title: Gold Foil Experiment
description: Formulated the Rutherford nuclear atomic model after directing the Geiger-Marsden gold foil scattering experiment, discovering the nucleus.

title: First Artificial Nuclear Fission
description: Succeeded in transforming nitrogen atoms into oxygen atoms by bombarding them with alpha particles, initiating the field of modern nuclear reactions.

title: Named the Proton
description: Postulated the existence of the proton as the fundamental positive charge building block, naming the hydrogen nucleus.

title: Death
description: Rutherford passed away in London and was laid to rest in Westminster Abbey near Newton and Kelvin.
